api/v3/handlers/billingprofiles.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for billing profile CRUD; bridges billing.Service (ProfileService) to HTTP with a domain-specific error encoder and goverter-generated conversions for workflow configs, tax config, and supplier parties.

patterns.name: Domain error encoder chained after GenericErrorEncoder
patterns.description: Every operation appends both apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() AND the local errorEncoder(); the local one must come last so it maps billing.NotFoundError->404, billing.ValidationError->400, billing.UpdateAfterDeleteError->409.

patterns.name: Goverter context params for namespace/ID injection
patterns.description: Generated FromAPI* funcs take a context param (namespace string or namespacedID models.NamespacedID) mapped via goverter:context + NamespaceFromContext/ResolveIDFromContext helpers instead of manual struct construction.

patterns.name: ProfileExpand{Apps:true} on read paths
patterns.description: GetBillingProfile and ListBillingProfiles always set billing.ProfileExpand{Apps: true} so app references populate in the response.

patterns.name: TaxConfig dual-field wrappers
patterns.description: toAPIBillingTaxConfigFull/fromAPIBillingTaxConfigFull wrap the goverter ToAPI/FromAPIBillingTaxConfig to keep new TaxCode and deprecated TaxCodeId fields in sync (TaxCode.Id takes precedence on input).

patterns.name: FromAPIBillingWorkflow defaults + discriminated alignment
patterns.description: FromAPIBillingWorkflow starts from billing.DefaultWorkflowConfig, parses ISO durations via period.Parse with fallback, and handles both subscription and anchored collection-alignment union variants.
patterns.example: if parsed, err := period.Parse(*workflow.Collection.Interval); err == nil { collInterval = datetime.ISODuration{Period: parsed} }

key_file_guides.role: Goverter variable declarations + hand-written helpers (ToAPI/FromAPIBillingWorkflow, ToAPI/FromAPIBillingParty, ToAPIBillingAppReference) and TaxConfig dual-field wrappers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromAPIBillingWorkflow uses period.Parse and falls back to billing.DefaultWorkflowConfig; both alignment variants (subscription, anchored) must be handled.

key_file_guides.role: Maps billing.NotFoundError/ValidationError/UpdateAfterDeleteError/ValidationIssue/AppError to HTTP status codes via commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters: more specific types appear before more general ones; uses billing.EncodeValidationIssues for the validation-bearing types.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by goverter (//go:build !goverter). Holds FromAPICreate/UpsertBillingProfileRequest, ToAPIBillingProfile(s), ToAPI/FromAPIBillingTaxConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT; regenerate after any convert.go annotation change with go generate.

key_file_guides.role: GetBillingProfile with ProfileExpand{Apps:true}; returns apierrors.NewNotFoundError when the service returns a nil profile.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Nil profile after the service call must return apierrors.NewNotFoundError, not a generic error.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (List/Create/Get/Update/Delete) and handler struct holding resolveNamespace closure, billing.Service, and options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses the resolveNamespace func(ctx)(string,error) closure pattern; new ops must call h.resolveNamespace, not a namespace decoder.

anti_patterns: Omitting errorEncoder() from a billing profile operation — GenericErrorEncoder alone won't map billing.NotFoundError to 404
Forgetting ProfileExpand{Apps:true} on read paths needing app reference data
Adding a new workflow field to convert.go without updating both ToAPIBillingWorkflow and FromAPIBillingWorkflow

decisions.decision: A local errorEncoder() runs alongside GenericErrorEncoder
decisions.rationale: Billing defines its own domain error types (NotFoundError, ValidationError, UpdateAfterDeleteError) that the generic models.Generic* encoder does not handle.

decisions.decision: Goverter context params thread namespace/namespacedID into generated converters
decisions.rationale: Avoids boilerplate in every FromAPI function while keeping the generated code auditable.

api/v3/handlers/currencies.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for listing/creating custom currencies and cost bases; resolves namespace via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder and delegates to currencies.CurrencyService.

patterns.name: NamespaceDecoder instead of resolveNamespace closure
patterns.description: This package holds a namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder and calls h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx) returning (string, bool) — not a func(ctx)(string,error). A false ok returns apierrors.NewInternalError.
patterns.example: ns, ok := h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx); if !ok { return CreateCurrencyRequest{}, apierrors.NewInternalError(ctx, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace")) }

patterns.name: Page default size differs by operation
patterns.description: ListCurrencies defaults to page size 100 (currency catalog) while ListCostBases defaults to 20.

patterns.name: BillingCurrency union via generic constructor
patterns.description: convert.go builds the v3.BillingCurrency discriminated union with NewBillingCurrencyFrom[T] calling the generated FromBillingCurrencyCustom/FromBillingCurrencyFiat methods.
patterns.example: func NewBillingCurrencyFrom[T v3.BillingCurrencyCustom | v3.BillingCurrencyFiat](v T) (v3.BillingCurrency, error)

patterns.name: Custom-vs-fiat routing by empty ID
patterns.description: ToAPIBillingCurrency treats any currency with c.ID == "" as fiat and otherwise custom.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (ListCurrencies, CreateCurrency, CreateCostBasis, ListCostBases) + handler struct holding namespaceDecoder, currencies.CurrencyService, options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder, not a closure; new ops must call GetNamespace (returns bool, not error).

key_file_guides.role: ListCurrencies with optional filter[type] and filter[code], sort parsing via request.ParseSortBy, page default 100.
key_file_guides.watch_for: params.Filter is optional — nil-check before dereferencing Type/Code.

key_file_guides.role: FromAPIBillingCurrencyType, NewBillingCurrencyFrom[T], ToAPIBillingCurrency, ToAPIBillingCostBasis (Rate Decimal -> string).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Custom vs fiat is decided solely by c.ID != ""; an empty ID is always treated as fiat.

key_file_guides.role: ListCostBases with a custom ListCostBasesArgs (CurrencyID + Params) and optional filter[fiat_code].
key_file_guides.watch_for: Default page size here is 20 (not 100); FilterFiatCode is built only when Params.Filter.FiatCode is non-nil.

anti_patterns: Using the resolveNamespace closure pattern instead of namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace
Defaulting ListCurrencies page size to 20 (use 100 for the currency catalog)
Mixing currency/cost-basis conversion logic into operation files instead of convert.go
Ignoring the ok bool from GetNamespace and assuming a valid namespace

decisions.decision: namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder is injected instead of a resolveNamespace closure
decisions.rationale: currencies was wired before the closure pattern was standardized; both resolve namespace from context, the decoder pattern is the older convention.

api/v3/handlers/customers/billing.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for customer-scoped billing: reading/writing the billing profile override, app-specific customer data (Stripe, CustomInvoicing, Sandbox), and Stripe checkout/portal sessions. Every mutation must guard against deleted customers.

patterns.name: Deleted-customer guard in the decoder
patterns.description: Mutation decoders call h.customerService.GetCustomer and return apierrors.NewGoneError when cus.IsDeleted() is true before building the domain input.
patterns.example: if cus.IsDeleted() { return ..., apierrors.NewGoneError(ctx, errors.New("customer is deleted")) }

patterns.name: App-type switch kept in sync across read/write
patterns.description: Handlers reading/writing app-specific data switch on application.GetType() over app.AppTypeStripe/AppTypeCustomInvoicing/AppTypeSandbox, returning apierrors.NewInternalError for unknown types; get_billing.go and update_billing.go must stay in sync.
patterns.example: switch application.GetType() { case app.AppTypeStripe: ... default: return resp, apierrors.NewInternalError(ctx, fmt.Errorf("unsupported app type")) }

patterns.name: Goverter for v3-to-legacy enum conversions
patterns.description: convert.go declares goverter variable-set FromAPI*/ToAPI* funcs with goverter:enum:map annotations (and goverter:enum:unknown @error); convert.gen.go is generated and never hand-edited.

patterns.name: Domain error encoder appended after generic
patterns.description: error_encoder.go matches billing.NotFoundError->404, ValidationError->400, UpdateAfterDeleteError->409, AppError->400 and is always appended AFTER apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder().

patterns.name: Stripe app resolved via billing profile
patterns.description: Checkout session creation resolves the Stripe AppID through h.billingService.ResolveStripeAppIDFromBillingProfile; portal session uses GetCustomerApp with an AppType filter — never hardcode app IDs.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface + handler struct (billingService, customerService, stripeService) and New().
key_file_guides.watch_for: handler.appService is declared but NOT set in New(); adding app.Service to New()'s signature breaks existing call sites.

key_file_guides.role: Source of truth for all v3-to-legacy and domain conversions; goverter annotations drive convert.gen.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a TypeSpec enum variant without a matching goverter:enum:map causes a runtime 'unexpected enum element' error.
DO NOT EDIT; overwritten on next go generate.

key_file_guides.role: Package-local encoder mapping billing-specific error types to HTTP codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must be appended AFTER GenericErrorEncoder; reversing order makes billing.ValidationError fall through to 500.

key_file_guides.role: Upserts the billing profile override AND app customer data; update_billing_app_data.go handles app-data-only updates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Shares the app-type switch with get_billing.go/update_billing_app_data.go — keep all in sync when adding an app type.

anti_patterns: Calling billing domain services without checking customer.IsDeleted() in the decoder
Hand-editing convert.gen.go instead of updating convert.go annotations and regenerating
Hardcoding a Stripe app ID instead of resolving via ResolveStripeAppIDFromBillingProfile
Adding app-type handling in only one of get_billing.go / update_billing.go

decisions.decision: Stripe checkout/portal session endpoints live alongside generic billing profile endpoints
decisions.rationale: They share the customer-scoped path prefix and identical deleted-customer validation; collocating avoids duplicating that validation.

decisions.decision: Goverter (with @error on unknown) drives v3-to-legacy enum conversion
decisions.rationale: The Stripe checkout enum space is large (10+ enums); generated mapping gives compile-time safety and avoids manual drift.
